test_output_summary: 583 passed, 55 warnings in 2.92s. The 4 new test classes (TestJ002016AvailableCapabilitiesPlaceholder, TestJ002016ToolUsageSection, TestJ002016Phase1ScopePreserved, TestJ002016BuildSupervisorFormatCompat) cover AC-001..AC-005 and pass cleanly. Existing tests (test_prompts.py legacy classes, test_supervisor.py, test_supervisor_no_llm_call.py, test_smoke_end_to_end.py, test_sessions.py) all still pass after the {available_capabilities} placeholder was added because build_supervisor was extended with a safe default. Ruff lint + format clean; mypy clean.
implementation_notes: Added a new '## Available Capabilities' section (containing the {available_capabilities} placeholder) and a '## Tool Usage' preference-list section to SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT, inserted between the Attended-Conversation Posture and Trace Richness sections per AC-001. The Tool Usage list mirrors design §10 verbatim in spirit: prefer calculate over mental arithmetic, list_available_capabilities at most once per session, prefer dispatch_by_capability over repeating specialist work, queue_build only when a feature is explicitly named, and return structured-error strings as-is. Phase 1 content (Identity, Attended-Conversation Posture, Model-Selection Philosophy, Trace Richness) is preserved verbatim line-for-line — verified by TestJ002016Phase1ScopePreserved. The {domain_prompt} placeholder remains the last templated value at the very bottom of the prompt under '## Domain-Specific Instructions'. Updated the module docstring to describe the new placeholder and to remove queue_build from the now-out-of-date forbidden list (queue_build is in scope for FEAT-JARVIS-002 per AC-002 and DDR-009). Added a minimal _DEFAULT_AVAILABLE_CAPABILITIES = 'No capabilities currently registered.' to jarvis.agents.supervisor and threaded it into the .format() call so existing build_supervisor callers keep working without raising KeyError; TASK-J002-017 will replace this default with rendered CapabilityDescriptor.as_prompt_block() output. Updated tests/test_prompts.py: removed queue_build from FORBIDDEN_TERMS (now allowed by AC-003 scope), added start_async_task to FORBIDDEN_TERMS (still out of scope per AC-003), and updated the format-resolution test to include all three placeholders.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] Gains a `## Tool Usage` section with the preference list from design §10 (prefer calculate over mental arithmetic; list_available_capabilities at most once per session; prefer dispatch_by_capability over repeating specialist work; queue_build only when feature explicitly named; return structured-error strings as-is).
completion_promises.evidence: Added a '## Tool Usage' section containing five bullet preferences directly mirroring design §10: (1) prefer `calculate` over mental arithmetic, (2) call `list_available_capabilities` at most once per session, (3) prefer `dispatch_by_capability` over repeating specialist work in-process, (4) use `queue_build` only when the user's request explicitly names a feature, (5) return structured-error strings as-is. Verified by all 7 tests in TestJ002016ToolUsageSection.
